text: Risk management is an important process for any successful project. It starts with identifying risks, which means thinking about what could go wrong, such as delays or budget problems. Once risks are known, you can develop mitigation strategies to reduce their impact. For example, you might adjust the timeline or allocate extra resources. Even with these plans, things can still fail. That is why you also need contingency plans. These are backup actions to take if a risk actually happens, like using a supplier on standby. Finally, you must keep monitoring risks throughout the project. Regular check-ins help you spot new problems early and adjust your strategies as needed.
